question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user write a crafted file to /tmp/alive with a manipulated modification timestamp to fool the age > 2*time.Minute liveness check, causing the healthcheck to report the pinger as alive while it is silently failing to submit transactions, masking ongoing transaction tampering?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user create /tmp/ready on a shared or misconfigured container filesystem before the pinger has successfully initialized its operator key, causing the readiness probe to pass and transactions to be submitted with an uninitialized or default operator identity?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user delete /tmp/alive to force the liveness probe to fail and trigger a container restart, causing in-flight transactions submitted by submitWithRetry() to be abandoned mid-retry cycle without confirmation, leading to double-spend ambiguity?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user with write access to /tmp continuously touch /tmp/alive to keep the liveness probe passing while simultaneously blocking the pinger's network access, causing the healthcheck to report healthy while all submitted transactions are silently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user replace /tmp/alive with a symlink pointing to a world-writable file outside /tmp, causing os.Stat() to follow the symlink and return attacker-controlled modification times, allowing the liveness check to be permanently spoofed while transactions are tampered with?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user replace /tmp/ready with a symlink to an always-existing system file, causing the readiness probe to permanently pass even after the pinger process has crashed and stopped submitting transactions, hiding the absence of transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between os.WriteFile('/tmp/alive', ...) in pinger/main.go and os.Stat('/tmp/alive') in healthcheck/main.go to observe a window where the file is partially written, causing the liveness check to fail and restart the pinger mid-transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user mount a FUSE filesystem over /tmp to intercept os.Stat('/tmp/alive') calls and return arbitrary modification times, allowing the attacker to control liveness probe outcomes and mask transaction submission failures?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user fill the /tmp filesystem to capacity so that os.WriteFile('/tmp/alive', ...) fails silently in pinger/main.go, causing the liveness probe to eventually report stale heartbeat and restart the pinger, interrupting pending transactions submitted via submitWithRetry()?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that os.Exit(1) is called without any cleanup when /tmp/ready is missing, causing abrupt termination of the healthcheck process that leaves in-flight transactions in an unknown state with no rollback mechanism?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of file locking on /tmp/alive to write a stale timestamp into the file concurrently with the pinger's heartbeat goroutine, causing the age > 2*time.Minute check to fail and trigger a liveness-induced restart that aborts pending transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that /tmp/ready and /tmp/alive have 0644 permissions (set by os.WriteFile with 0644 and 0o644) to overwrite these files from another process in the same container, manipulating probe outcomes to mask transaction submission tampering?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/cmd/healthcheck/main.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a shared /tmp volume between containers in a pod to write /tmp/alive from a sidecar container, keeping the liveness probe passing for the pinger container even after the pinger process has been killed and is no longer submitting transactions?
